title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, Draining, and Recovery · Inference Brief: Api Compatibility Evolution
summary: Documentation differs from runtime, examples contain secrets, additive changes break strict clients, storage contracts before old revisions drain, and rollback cannot restore invariants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts api compatibility evolution increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, ordering, status, errors, authentication, caching, timing, and business behavior.
Choose a source of truth and gate generated clients, runtime validation, examples, operation IDs, security behavior, unknown fields, and spec-to-service drift.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Api Compatibility Evolution · Theory Api Compatibility Evolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2452 in a canary revision draining long-lived response streams. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate one contract-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and recoverable release; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, ordering, status, errors, authentication, caching, timing, and business behavior.
